key_insight: PPI-GD reduces gradient oracle cost by interpolating first-order information over patches of the data domain, exploiting smoothness in the training data rather than only in the optimization variables.
engineering_relevance: This can lower training-query overhead for large ERM problems when data features are sufficiently smooth and the data dimension is modest enough to support interpolation-based approximations.
method: Query the first-order oracle at equidistant points in the data domain.
Build piecewise tensor-product polynomial interpolants of sampled gradients over local patches.
Use the interpolated gradient as an inexact update in gradient descent and analyze oracle complexity for strongly convex and non-convex losses.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports oracle-complexity analysis, with comparisons showing PPI-GD can outperform several GD variants in key regimes under sufficient smoothness assumptions.
watch_item: Performance depends on strong smoothness and a data-space dimension bounded by a polylogarithmic function of sample size, so scalability may be limited when these conditions fail.
paper_title: On the Oracle Complexity of Interpolation-Based Gradient Descent
